generated_for_sub_pass: §1.2 Supabase Implementation 0.3 — Conditional Live Client Initialization
auth_storage_scope: Supabase auth state only for PKCE verifier continuity and Supabase session guard evaluation

client_initialization_behavior.absent_config: Returns a bounded absent_config / not_initialized state without loading the Supabase module.
client_initialization_behavior.present_config: Loads the exact version-pinned browser ESM module and calls createClient with only SUPABASE_URL and the preferred or fallback public key.
client_initialization_behavior.create_client_scope: Bounded to conditional browser client initialization and Supabase-managed auth storage for PKCE verifier/session continuity only; no database, credential, protected-route admission verification, application-data persistence, or authenticated-surface birth behavior is implemented.
client_initialization_behavior.oauth_flow_alignment: The browser client is configured with auth.flowType pkce so provider redirects align with the existing query-code callback contract.
client_initialization_behavior.auth_storage_boundary: The browser client permits Supabase-managed auth storage through persistSession true, with autoRefreshToken and detectSessionInUrl remaining false and no custom storage adapter.

claimable_after_this_pass: A conditional Supabase browser-client initialization path exists using the selected static ESM/runtime-public-config strategy; it initializes only when externally supplied browser-safe runtime public config is present.
Bounded Supabase-managed auth storage is implemented for PKCE verifier continuity and Supabase auth-session guard continuity only.

next_threshold.name: §1.2 Supabase Implementation 0.4 — Auth Callback Boundary or Login Surface Authorization
next_threshold.purpose: Define the next separately authorized auth boundary before any credential capture, callback handling, protected route, authenticated shell, or database behavior.

hard_boundaries_preserved: No package installation or package files are introduced.
No Supabase runtime public config values are committed.
No runtime config file with values is committed.

No auth calls beyond already bounded provider/callback/session-guard methods, credential capture, backend, database access, application-data persistence, RLS, tenant isolation, customer workspace, billing, Palisade, Weave runtime, or public NEXUS runtime is implemented.
Server-secret names remain bounded to validators and data records as forbidden terms.
